content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 20, 2002


Joseph V.A. Partansky
1406 Barbis Way
Concord, CA 94518

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-221

Dear Mr. Partansky:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ You ask about the legal responsibilities of the city regarding use of candidate statements and the duties of cable companies. While the Act requires that the Commission provide formal written advice or informal assistance to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative, the Commission does not provide advice to a third party about a public official's duties under the Act. (Section 83114; regulation 18329, copy enclosed.) None of your questions appear to relate to your duties as a candidate for city clerk. Therefore, we decline to provide the assistance you have requested.

Enclosed, however, is some general information that you may find helpful. We have enclosed our manual on "Campaign Provisions of the Political Reform Act" and an informational pamphlet entitled, "Can I Vote? Conflict of Interest Overview."

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosures
LM:JWW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\02-221

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: The Commission does not provide advice to a third party about a public official's duties under the Act. None of the requester's questions appear to relate to their duties as a candidate for city clerk. Therefore, the Commission declines to provide the assistance requested.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked about the legal responsibilities of the city regarding use of candidate statements and the duties of cable companies under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the requester's questions do not relate to their own duties as a candidate and the Commission cannot provide advice about third parties' duties under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; information extracted from body of letter. Document clearly states decline of assistance per Section 83114 and regulation 18329. Some general informational materials were enclosed but no substantive advice provided.

QUESTION: The requester asked about the legal responsibilities of the city regarding use of candidate statements and the duties of cable companies under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The Commission does not provide advice to a third party about a public official's duties under the Act. None of the requester's questions appear to relate to their duties as a candidate for city clerk. Therefore, the Commission declines to provide the assistance requested.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines a request for advice from a candidate for city clerk regarding city responsibilities for candidate statements and cable company duties, as such questions do not relate to the requester's own duties and the FPPC cannot advise third parties about public officials' duties.
